headline.text: L1 S3 metal detector recorded *20+ openings* in short window — maintenance on site, root cause unknown.

summary: Three active quality concerns: L1 S3 metal detector fault generating high reject rate, L3 MFM running 33 pieces per bag to compensate for underweight truffles (spec is 32), and L3 ML4 missing hardware on two lines. Two chocolate barrels from L3 held at L1 due to scrap-tag cross-contamination.

holds.title: Two chocolate barrels from L3 held for scrap
holds.summary: Barrels arrived with orange scrap tags and empty tags mixed, indicating scrap barrel reused for clean chocolate; barrels cannot be used and will be scrapped.

food_safety.title: Correct enrober infeed pipe found on floor
food_safety.summary: S3 enrober infeed pipe found on floor between wash area and S2 ingredients; washed, sanitized, ATP passed, staged on table for installation after next reheat.
food_safety.status: Resolved — cleaned, verified, staged for reinstallation.

food_safety.title: ML4 TG5 missing bolts on cleaning rollers
food_safety.summary: Two out of five rollers missing bolts, one very loose — potential fastener-into-product risk during weekend clean.
food_safety.status: Open, maintenance requested to follow up.

food_safety.title: ML4 TG3 scraper missing
food_safety.summary: Scraper not reinstalled after cleaning; flagged at end of Level 2 clean, maintenance requested for follow-up today.
food_safety.status: Open, maintenance assigned to address today.

quality.title: S3 metal detector generating high reject rate
quality.summary: Over 20 metal detector openings in short period; maintenance on site, no root cause identified yet.

quality.title: MFM running 33 pieces per bag instead of spec 32
quality.summary: Individual truffle weights varying low (10.35–14.35 g, min 11 g); line dosing 33 pieces to meet total bag weight after checkweigher rejected 32-piece bags.
quality.status: Line running 33 pieces; bags passing weight check, operator trained to adjust target based on reject counts.

quality.title: S3 elevated pouch scrap from dirty pouches
quality.summary: Higher than normal pouch scrap due to dirty pouches and printer head issues between 16:00–17:00; printer cleaned and resolved, dirty-pouch issue persists, maintenance inspecting.
quality.status: Printer issue resolved; dirty-pouch cause under investigation.

quality.title: MFM 21.56 lbs flow-wrap defects on two pallets
quality.summary: First shift QA found 11 lbs open/doubled/empty pouches on one pallet, 10.56 lbs on second pallet.
quality.status: Scrap recorded; no root cause reported.

quality.title: ML4 undercount master cases
quality.summary: Two master cases inspected contained 932 and 931 pieces; spec minimum is 955 pieces per case.
quality.status: Open, no disposition or root cause reported.

opportunities.text: Consider adding the S3 enrober infeed pipe to the post-reheat installation checklist — today's floor find suggests it was removed and not tracked back to service.
Consider adding fastener inspection to ML4 TG startup checklist — today's missing bolts on TG5 rollers and missing TG3 scraper both surfaced after Level 2 clean.
Review MFM individual-piece weight control limits — today's wide variance (10.35–14.35 g) forced line to run 33 pieces per bag instead of spec 32 to meet total weight.
